event: The Creation of Fish and Birds (Fifth Day)
symbols: Rays of light breaking through clouds — the creative Word of God commanding life into existence
Great sea creatures emerging from the deep — the teeming life God called forth from the waters
Birds in flight — creatures of the air, the second category of life created on the fifth day
Churning waters — the primordial deep from which God brings forth living things
description: A dramatic engraving depicts great sea monsters and serpentine creatures emerging from churning ocean waters. Massive scaled and coiling forms break the surface amid white foam and waves. Above, brilliant shafts of light pierce through dramatic clouds, illuminating flocks of birds in flight. Rocky cliffs frame the left side. The composition conveys immense power and creative energy through dynamic lines and strong contrasts of light and shadow.
theological_description: The fifth day of Creation (GEN.1.20-23) records God's command: 'Let the waters swarm with swarms of living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth.' God created the 'great sea creatures' (tanninim) and every living thing that moves in the waters, and every winged bird. The great sea monsters in this image recall not only the Genesis account but also the poetic descriptions of God's creative power over the deep in Job and the Psalms: 'There is the sea, great and wide, which teems with creatures innumerable... there go the ships, and Leviathan, which you formed to play in it' (PSA.104.25-26). The light from heaven represents the divine fiat — God's word that calls being out of nothing. The fifth day demonstrates God's delight in abundance and variety, blessing these creatures with the command to 'be fruitful and multiply' (GEN.1.22) — the first blessing pronounced in Scripture.

scripture_refs.reason: God creates the sea creatures and birds on the fifth day — the specific event depicted
God created the great sea creatures and every living thing that moves in the waters
The sea teeming with innumerable creatures, Leviathan playing in the deep
God's description of Leviathan as a manifestation of His untamable creative power
The first divine blessing: be fruitful and multiply — God's delight in the abundance of life
